content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Jun 14, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on June 14, 2026, focused on optimizing CI/CD workflows to improve testing efficiency. The team introduced path-based gating for expensive test lanes to streamline the PR process. Additionally, a significant number of pull requests were opened to address orchestration logic, core provider constraints, and plugin integration fixes.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**CI/CD Workflow Optimization**
* Implemented lightweight path classifiers in the Tests and Scenario PR E2E workflows to skip expensive lanes (server, client, plugin, desktop, zero-key, cloud) when the relevant code surface remains untouched.
* Maintained full-strength execution for manual and scheduled runs.
* Relevant PR: [#8408](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8408)

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
* [#8416](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8416): feat(plugin-agent-orchestrator): add ack progress mode + dedupe spawn acks.
* [#8415](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8415): fix(core): bound relationships provider output.
* [#8413](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8413): fix(plugin-agent-orchestrator): don't surface route-prefix or data-source URLs as the sub-agent deliverable.
* [#8412](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8412): fix(core): don't demote a model-chosen coding delegation to a simple direct reply.
* [#8411](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8411): fix(plugin-agent-orchestrator): route app-build spawns on the originating user request, not the planner's terse task.
* [#8410](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8410): fix(plugin-agent-orchestrator): route app-build spawns on the originating user request, not the planner's terse task.
* [#8409](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8409): fix(plugin-codex-cli): make the codex planner work with the native-tools planner.

## CLOSED ISSUES
* None.

## NEW ISSUES
* None.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
* None.
